result:
All confirmed directly against source (grep/read), cbm graph query for this field returned nothing so I relied on source verification.

## Per-runqueue lock field

**`struct rq`** — `kernel/sched/sched.h:962`
**Field:** `raw_spinlock_t __lock;` — `kernel/sched/sched.h:964` (comment above it: `/* runqueue lock: */`, line 963)

Note the v6.6 twist: it's no longer taken with plain `raw_spin_lock(&rq->lock)` as in older kernels. The field was renamed to `__lock` (double-underscore, "don't touch directly") specifically because of **core scheduling** (`CONFIG_SCHED_CORE`): when core scheduling is enabled, sibling hyperthreads share one lock (`rq->core->__lock`) instead of each rq having its own. So the actual lock to take is resolved dynamically via a helper, not the field directly.

## Primary helper functions

Indirection layer in `kernel/sched/sched.h`:
- `rq_lockp(struct rq *rq)` — `sched.h:1235` (CONFIG_SCHED_CORE) and a second, simpler definition at `sched.h:1333` (`!CONFIG_SCHED_CORE`, just returns `&rq->__lock`)
- `__rq_lockp(struct rq *rq)` — `sched.h:1243` (CONFIG_SCHED_CORE) / `sched.h:1338` (`!CONFIG_SCHED_CORE`)
  - Both pick `&rq->core->__lock` if core scheduling is active for that rq, else `&rq->__lock`.

Actual lock/unlock helpers, declared `sched.h:1366-1399`, **defined** in `kernel/sched/core.c`:
- `void raw_spin_rq_lock_nested(struct rq *rq, int subclass)` — `core.c:551`
  Loops: disables preempt, resolves `__rq_lockp(rq)`, takes it, and re-checks the pointer didn't change (rq could switch which lock backs it when core scheduling toggles) — retries if it did.
- `bool raw_spin_rq_trylock(struct rq *rq)` — `core.c:576` (same retry-on-pointer-change pattern)
- `void raw_spin_rq_unlock(struct rq *rq)` — `core.c:600` → `raw_spin_unlock(rq_lockp(rq))`

Thin wrappers built on these (`sched.h:1370-1399`):
- `raw_spin_rq_lock(rq)` → calls `raw_spin_rq_lock_nested(rq, 0)`
- `raw_spin_rq_lock_irq(rq)`, `raw_spin_rq_unlock_irq(rq)`
- `_raw_spin_rq_lock_irqsave(rq)` / `raw_spin_rq_unlock_irqrestore(rq, flags)`

## Two real call sites in `kernel/sched/core.c`

1. `raw_spin_rq_lock(rq);` — **`kernel/sched/core.c:2689`**, in `push_cpu_stop()`, taken right after `p->pi_lock` to safely re-check `task_rq(p) != rq`.
2. `raw_spin_rq_lock_irq(rq);` — **`kernel/sched/core.c:6381`**, end of the core-scheduling steal-cookie-task scan loop, re-acquiring the rq lock (IRQ-disabling variant) before continuing.

(Also worth noting: `core.c:616` in `double_rq_lock()`, `core.c:9264`, and `core.c:9516` are other direct call sites of `raw_spin_rq_lock()` if you want more examples.)
